title: Ship Loss Body
problem: Ship Loss onset is now much better, but its body segment remains too bright and too extended versus the measured Galaga death body window.
strategy: Capture baseline, hand-designed candidates, and reference subclip windows through the live browser audio engine, compare against the canonical application-guide reference window, and promote only candidates that clear explicit keeper gates.

capturePlan.rationale: Short cue captures include a small recorder preroll so onset analysis reflects the cue rather than MediaRecorder startup timing.

successMeasure: A keeper must improve whole-cue risk and onset/body/tail segment risk while preserving scheduled loss-phrase duration, semantic role coverage, and band/centroid guardrails.

summary.resolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail
summary.source: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass

decision.reason: No Ship Loss Body candidate cleared whole-cue, segment, duration, band, centroid, and role-match gates.

nextStep: Do not promote Ship Loss Body yet; use the measured best candidate to refine the generator or scoring gates.
